name: sys
conference: Systems / AI Infra / AI Compiler / RISC-V

papers.title: TuxBot: Semantic-Aware Online OS Tuning with Large Language Models
authors: Georgios Liargkovas, Mihir Nitin Joshi, Hubertus Franke, Kostis Kaffes
papers.abstract:
arXiv:2605.15026v2 Announce Type: replace 
Abstract: Online OS tuning can improve long-running services, but existing controllers are poorly matched to live hosts. They treat scheduler, power, memory, and I/O controls as black-box variables and optimize a scalar reward. This view ignores cross-knob policy structure, breaks down when application metrics are unavailable, and can send a running service into degraded regions that persist after the bad setting is removed. We present TuxBot, a host-side framework for steady-state OS tuning with bounded language-model guidance. TuxBot turns knob schemas, telemetry, current configuration, recent action--response history, and retrieved prior runs into a compact decision context.
